question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase. This is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution. The market question asks if CPI will rise more than 0.9% in March 2026, and with the official data showing only 0.3%, the market must resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data released April 10, 2026 shows March CPI increased 0.3%
0.3% is substantially below the 0.9% threshold needed for YES resolution
Multiple credible sources confirm the 0.3% reading
Market closes April 10 after data release, so outcome is already determined
risks: Potential data revision (though unlikely to be large enough to change outcome)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria (though language is clear)
